title: SDD example rules, schemas, and documentation
description:
Ship SDD as example rules, schemas, and docs, the first application of the engine, with description criteria canonical and labels derived.

## Background

SDD is configuration, not code. The examples demonstrate falsifiable, traceable specs and prove the engine is methodology-agnostic. See `dev/active/f6a704d0-validation-engine-plan.md` (step 12) and decision record section 10.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] [hard] An example `.jit/rules.toml` plus `.jit/schemas/` demonstrate `req:`/`satisfies:` coverage and reference-integrity, with description `[hard]`/`[aspirational]` criteria as the canonical source and `req:`/`satisfies:` labels derived.
- [ ] [hard] A spec content schema validates the requirement/scenario structure and the `[hard]`/`[aspirational]` criteria check.
- [ ] [hard] Documentation explains authoring rules and positions the engine as methodology-agnostic with at least one non-SDD example.

## Notes

Builds on the validate surface, graph rules, and shorthand desugaring.
